summary: Completed comprehensive backend and frontend testing of Research Pilot AI research execution system with PostgreSQL/Redis/WebSocket architecture. Fixed cascade delete bug in Plan model. All 14 backend API tests pass. Frontend UI fully functional with Dashboard, Workspace, Navigator, Inspector, Task Graph, and Agent Graph components.

fixed.issue: Cascade delete failing - Plan relationship missing cascade='all, delete-orphan'
fixed.fix: Added cascade='all, delete-orphan' to Plan relationship in models.py

passed_tests: Health check endpoint returns 200 with PostgreSQL/Redis/WebSocket features
List projects returns array of projects
Get existing project returns project details with task_counts
Get non-existent project returns 404
Create project creates project with plan and tasks
Delete project removes project and cascades to plan/tasks
List project tasks returns tasks with 'state' field
Task graph endpoint returns React Flow nodes and edges
Agent graph endpoint returns agent orchestration nodes
Stats endpoint returns project/task/artifact/paper counts
List artifacts endpoint works
List papers endpoint works
Execute-all endpoint starts pipeline execution
Export formats endpoint returns available formats
Dashboard loads with Welcome message and stats
Project cards display with status badges
Navigator shows Projects, Tasks, Documents, Literature sections
Workspace loads with 3-panel layout
Task Graph tab renders React Flow visualization
Agent Graph tab renders n8n-style orchestration view
Inspector panel shows item metadata
Execute Pipeline button functional
WebSocket connection established for real-time updates
critical_code_review_comments: ESLint warning in Workspace.jsx: useEffect missing 'fetchGraphs' dependency - minor, doesn't affect functionality
context_for_next_testing_agent: PostgreSQL/Redis/WebSocket architecture fully functional. All CRUD operations work. Task execution pipeline starts correctly. WebSocket real-time updates working. React Flow graphs render properly. The cascade delete bug was fixed by adding cascade='all, delete-orphan' to the Plan relationship in Project model.
rca_of_the_issue: Cascade Delete Bug: When deleting a project, SQLAlchemy was trying to set Plan.project_id to NULL instead of deleting the Plan. Root cause: Plan relationship in Project model was missing cascade='all, delete-orphan'. Fix: Added cascade option to ensure Plans are deleted when their parent Project is deleted.
